error.signature: Emails sent via API land in spam despite valid SPF and DKIM because of alignment failure

verdict.summary: DMARC requires 'alignment': the domain in SPF/DKIM must match the From header domain. Sending from user@example.com via SendGrid (SPF passes for sendgrid.net, not example.com) fails DMARC alignment even though SPF itself passes.

dead_ends.action: Set up SPF and DKIM and assume emails won't be marked as spam
dead_ends.why_fails: SPF and DKIM can both pass independently but DMARC still fails if neither aligns with the From domain. SPF must pass for the From domain, not just the envelope sender.

dead_ends.action: Use email service provider's default sending domain
dead_ends.why_fails: Sending from user@example.com via service's domain means SPF passes for the service domain, not yours. DMARC alignment fails.

workarounds.action: Configure custom DKIM signing with your domain on the email service provider
workarounds.how: Add CNAME records for DKIM: selector._domainkey.example.com -> service-provided-value. This makes DKIM align with your From domain.

workarounds.action: Set up custom Return-Path domain for SPF alignment
workarounds.how: Configure bounce subdomain: bounces.example.com CNAME to service's domain. Envelope sender aligns with From domain.

workarounds.action: Start with DMARC p=none to monitor before enforcing
workarounds.how: v=DMARC1; p=none; rua=mailto:dmarc@example.com  # monitor alignment failures before setting p=reject
